2606.13380.title: An LLM System for Autonomous Variational Quantum Circuit Design
2606.13380.paper_summary: The design of high performing quantum circuits remains largely dependent on human expertise. We introduce an autonomous agentic framework that employs large language models (LLMs) to conduct iterative quantum circuit designs under explicit design constraints. Our system integrates seven components: Exploration, Generation, Discussion, Validation, Storage, Evaluation, and Review. These components form a closed-loop workflow that combines web-based knowledge acquisition, literature-grounded critique, executable code generation, and experimental feedback. We evaluate the framework on two tasks: quantum feature map construction for quantum machine learning and ansatz generation for variational quantum eigensolver applications in quantum chemistry. In image classification benchmarks, the best generated feature map outperforms representative quantum feature maps and, when scaled to larger qubit counts, surpasses the classical radial basis function kernel. In molecular ground state estimation across seven molecules, the generated ansatz attains competitive accuracy with widely used chemically inspired and hardware-efficient constructions while satisfying the imposed scaling constraints. These results establish LLM driven agentic system as a viable paradigm for automated quantum circuit design and illustrate how AI systems can participate in iterative scientific optimization workflows across scientific domains.
2606.13380.paper_summary_zh: 高效能量子電路的設計仍然在很大程度上依賴於人類專家的知識。<lb/>我們提出了一個自主代理框架，利用大型語言模型（LLMs）在明確的設計約束下進行迭代的量子電路設計。<lb/>我們的系統整合了七個組件：探索、生成、討論、驗證、存儲、評估和審查。<lb/>這些組件形成了一個閉環工作流程，結合了基於網絡的知識獲取、文獻基礎的批評、可執行代碼生成和實驗反饋。<lb/>我們在兩個任務上評估了這個框架：量子機器學習的量子特徵圖構建和變分量子特徵求解器在量子化學中的ansatz生成。<lb/>在圖像分類基準測試中，最佳生成的特徵圖超越了代表性的量子特徵圖，並且在擴展到更大的量子比特數量時，超過了經典的徑向基函數核。<lb/>在七種分子的分子基態估計中，生成的ansatz在準確性上達到了與廣泛使用的化學啟發和硬體高效構造的競爭水平，同時滿足了施加的擴展約束。<lb/>這些結果確立了以LLM驅動的代理系統作為自動化量子電路設計的可行範式，並展示了AI系統如何參與跨科學領域的迭代科學優化工作流程。
2606.13380.author: Kenya Sakka et.al.
2606.13380.authors: Kenya Sakka, Wataru Mizukami, Kosuke Mitarai
